fixes.description: T004 (afb5 + picloram): comparison_baseline WT->mutant, expected_direction decreased->increased.
fixes.reason: Combined KO+treatment test was encoded with baseline=WT but the validator auto-applies mutant-baseline for tests with both gene_modifier and exogenous_supply. The validator's ratio=1.15 previously read as a wrong-direction failure; under the correct mutant baseline, picloram still elicits residual elongation via the remaining TIR1/AFB paralogs in afb5 background, so relative to afb5 alone the phenotype is 'increased' (reduced-sensitivity, not absent).
fixes.biological_justification: Prigge et al. 2020 (eLife) reports afb5-5 plants have decreased sensitivity to picloram-induced hypocotyl elongation, but not full insensitivity - picloram still causes significant elongation via residual TIR1/AFB1/2/3 receptors. Relative to the afb5 mutant alone (no picloram), the +picloram treatment gives an increased hypocotyl length.

fixes.description: T046 (ein2 + ACC): comparison_baseline WT->mutant, expected_direction increased->unchanged (Trap 5 signaling mutant rescue).
fixes.reason: §Trap 5 signaling-mutant rescue: ein2 is a signaling mutant; ACC is converted to ethylene but cannot be transduced because EIN2 is absent. Relative to ein2 alone (mutant baseline), ACC has no effect. Previous encoding produced validator ratio=1.0 with expected=increased, triggering a false failure.
fixes.biological_justification: EIN2 is the central transducer of ethylene signaling between CTR1 and EIN3. ein2 null mutants are fully ethylene-insensitive; ACC (ethylene precursor) cannot rescue because there is no EIN2 to cleave. Canonical Trap 5 pattern: receptor/transducer mutant + hormone = 'unchanged' from mutant baseline.

fixes.description: T047 (ein3 + ACC): comparison_baseline WT->mutant, expected_direction increased->unchanged (Trap 5).
fixes.reason: Same Trap 5 pattern as T046: EIN3 is the master ethylene-responsive TF; without EIN3, ACC-induced ethylene cannot execute the triple response. Expected 'unchanged' from ein3 mutant baseline.
fixes.biological_justification: EIN3 is the final TF in the ethylene signaling cascade. ein3 single mutants are largely ethylene-insensitive; ACC has no phenotypic effect without the downstream TF.

fixes.description: T049 (ein3 eil1 double + ACC): comparison_baseline WT->mutant, expected_direction increased->unchanged (Trap 5).
fixes.reason: Same Trap 5 pattern; ein3 eil1 double removes both redundant TFs completely, guaranteeing ACC cannot signal. Expected 'unchanged' from mutant baseline.
fixes.biological_justification: EIN3 and EIL1 are redundant ethylene-responsive TFs. The double mutant is fully insensitive to ACC/ethylene. Rescue impossible = unchanged vs mutant baseline.

fixes.description: T112 (tir1 + auxin treatment): comparison_baseline WT->mutant, expected_direction decreased->unchanged (Trap 5).
fixes.reason: Classical Trap 5 auxin signaling-mutant rescue: TIR1 KO abolishes auxin perception; exogenous auxin cannot signal without its receptor. Expected 'unchanged' from tir1 mutant baseline. Previous encoding baseline=WT + expected=decreased gave ratio=1.0 false failure.
fixes.biological_justification: TIR1 is the primary auxin receptor; tir1 null mutants show strongly reduced auxin response. Adding exogenous auxin to tir1 cannot rescue because the perception gate is closed.

results.reason: Best-method (Algebraic) improved 91.4%->95.7% (+4.3%), well above 0.5% threshold. All three methods improved (ODE +3.4%, RWR +0.8%). Kappa rose from 0.84 to 0.92, MCC from 0.68 to 0.90. DARS 12.02->13.22 (both exceptional band).
